theme: Procedural rooms-and-corridors generation with doors, stairs, and multi-floor descent
summary: Replace the empty/static floor from Sprint 1 with a seedable rooms-and-corridors procedural generator. Floors must contain non-overlapping rectangular rooms connected by corridors, doors at room/corridor boundaries, and up/down staircases that allow the player to descend through a multi-floor dungeon. Generation must be deterministic given a seed so runs are reproducible.
assumptions: Sprint 1 has produced a Python 3.11+ project scaffold with an entity/tile/grid model and a working ASCII render loop where the player can move on a single floor.
Sprint 1 exposes a Floor / Map / Tile abstraction the generator can populate, or Sprint 2 may introduce one that Sprint 1's render loop is adapted to consume.
Rendering is terminal-based (tcod, blessed, curses, or similar). Glyphs follow the spec: '#' walls, '.' floor, '+' door, '<' stairs up, '>' stairs down, '@' player.
The game can be launched with a CLI entrypoint (e.g. `python -m whisperdeep` or `python main.py`); a non-interactive / headless test mode is acceptable for criteria verification.

criteria.description: A dedicated dungeon generation module exists (e.g., `whisperdeep/dungeon/generator.py` or `whisperdeep/world/generator.py`) and is importable.
testSteps: Locate a Python module whose name contains 'generat' under the package root.
Import it: `python -c "import whisperdeep.<...>.generator"` (or equivalent) succeeds without error.
criteria.threshold: Module file exists and imports cleanly.

criteria.description: Generator exposes a callable that produces a Floor/Map given a width, height, and seed.
testSteps: Call the generator with width=80, height=40, seed=12345.
Verify the returned object exposes a 2D tile grid (or accessor) of the requested dimensions.
criteria.threshold: Returned map dimensions equal requested width/height; tile access works at all in-bounds coordinates.

criteria.description: Generation is deterministic: same seed + same dimensions produces an identical floor layout.
testSteps: Generate two floors with seed=42, width=60, height=30.
Compare every tile's type/glyph between the two floors.
criteria.threshold: 100% of tiles match across the two generations.

criteria.description: Different seeds produce different floor layouts (low-collision check).
testSteps: Generate floors with seeds 1, 2, 3, 4, 5 at the same dimensions.
Compute a hash of each tile grid and compare.
criteria.threshold: At least 4 out of 5 hashes are unique.

criteria.description: Generated floor contains multiple non-overlapping rectangular rooms.
testSteps: Generate a floor at 80x40 with a fixed seed.
Inspect the generator's room list (or detect rooms by flood-fill of floor tiles).
Verify there are at least 4 rooms.
Verify no two rooms' bounding rectangles overlap (interior tiles).
criteria.threshold: >=4 rooms, zero pairwise interior overlap.

criteria.description: Rooms are connected by corridors so all floor tiles are reachable from any other floor tile.
testSteps: Generate a floor.
Pick any floor tile and run BFS over walkable tiles (floor + door + stairs).
Compare reachable count to total walkable count.
criteria.threshold: 100% of walkable tiles are reachable from any starting walkable tile.

criteria.description: Doors are placed at the boundary between rooms and corridors.
testSteps: Generate a floor with a fixed seed.
Verify at least one door tile ('+') exists on the map.
For each door, verify it has at least one adjacent room-floor tile and at least one adjacent corridor or room-floor tile (i.e., it sits at a transition).
criteria.threshold: >=1 door per floor on average across 5 seeds; every door has valid adjacency.

criteria.description: Each non-final floor contains exactly one downstairs ('>'), and each non-first floor contains exactly one upstairs ('<').
testSteps: Generate a 3-floor dungeon.
Count '<' and '>' tiles per floor.
Verify floor 0: 0 upstairs, 1 downstairs. Floor 1: 1 upstairs, 1 downstairs. Floor 2 (final): 1 upstairs, 0 downstairs (or 1 if final-descent is allowed; the contract requires consistency).
criteria.threshold: Stair counts match the rule above for all floors.

criteria.description: Stairs are placed on walkable floor tiles (not embedded in walls).
testSteps: For each generated floor, find every '<' and '>' tile.
Verify each is reachable via BFS from the player spawn or from any other walkable tile.
criteria.threshold: All stair tiles are reachable.

criteria.description: Player spawns inside a room on a walkable floor tile.
testSteps: Generate a floor and create/spawn the player.
Inspect the tile at the player's position.
criteria.threshold: Player tile is a floor tile (walkable) and lies within one of the generated room rectangles.

criteria.description: Multi-floor descent: stepping onto '>' transitions the player to the next floor and places them on or near the upstairs.
testSteps: Start a game with a fixed seed.
Programmatically pathfind/teleport the player to '>'.
Trigger the descend action.
Verify the current floor index increments and the player's tile is now the upstairs '<' (or an adjacent walkable tile) on the new floor.
criteria.threshold: Floor index advances; player ends on '<' or an adjacent walkable tile.

criteria.description: Ascending: stepping onto '<' transitions the player back to the previous floor at the location of that floor's '>' (or adjacent walkable).
testSteps: After descending in C11, pathfind/teleport to '<'.
Trigger the ascend action.
Verify the floor index decrements and the player is at the previous floor's '>' tile (or adjacent walkable).
criteria.threshold: Floor index decrements; player ends on '>' or adjacent walkable tile.

criteria.description: Floor persistence across descent/ascent: the previous floor's layout is preserved when revisited.
testSteps: Capture floor 0's tile grid before descending.
Descend to floor 1, then ascend back to floor 0.
Capture floor 0's tile grid again and compare.
criteria.threshold: 100% of tiles identical before and after the round-trip.

criteria.description: World/run object supports at least 3 floors generated lazily or eagerly with distinct seeds derived from a master seed.
testSteps: Construct a World with master seed=999 and 3 floors.
Generate floor 0, 1, 2.
Verify each floor exists, is non-empty, and that floors 0 and 1 differ (hash compare).
criteria.threshold: All 3 floors generated; floor 0 != floor 1 by tile-hash.

criteria.description: Render output uses the documented glyph set: '#' walls, '.' floor, '+' doors, '<' upstairs, '>' downstairs, '@' player.
testSteps: Run the game in a non-interactive headless render mode (or call the renderer's frame-to-string function).
Verify each glyph type appears or is renderable; verify the player glyph '@' is visible at the player position.
criteria.threshold: All six glyphs are produced by the renderer when the corresponding tile/entity exists.

criteria.description: The render loop displays the generated dungeon (not just a blank/static floor) when the game starts.
testSteps: Launch the game with seed=1.
Capture the initial frame (snapshot or screenshot).
Verify it shows walls, floor, at least one room boundary, and the player glyph.
criteria.threshold: Frame contains '#', '.', and '@' glyphs in a recognizably room-shaped layout.

criteria.description: Player cannot walk through walls; bumping into '#' is a no-op (does not move the player).
testSteps: Place the player adjacent to a wall tile.
Issue a move command into the wall.
Verify the player's position is unchanged and the floor/turn state is consistent.
criteria.threshold: Player position unchanged after wall bump.

criteria.description: Player can move through doors ('+') as if they were floor tiles (or after a simple open action; either is acceptable as long as it's documented).
testSteps: Place the player adjacent to a door tile.
Issue a move command into the door.
Verify the player either moves onto the door tile or opens it then moves onto it.
criteria.threshold: Player traverses door successfully.

criteria.description: The game accepts a `--seed` CLI argument that controls dungeon generation.
testSteps: Run the game twice with `--seed 7` and capture each initial frame.
Run again with `--seed 8` and capture the frame.
Verify the two seed=7 frames are identical and that seed=7 differs from seed=8.
criteria.threshold: Same seed -> identical frames; different seed -> different frames.

criteria.description: Automated test suite covers generator determinism, connectivity, and stairs invariants.
testSteps: Locate test files under `tests/` covering the generator.
Run `pytest` (or the project's chosen runner).
criteria.threshold: Test suite includes >=5 tests targeting the dungeon generator and all of them pass.

criteria.description: Sprint 1 functionality is preserved: project still launches, the entity/tile/grid model still works, and basic player movement on a floor still functions.
testSteps: Launch the game.
Verify it starts without errors.
Verify the player glyph appears and at least one move command in a free direction succeeds.
criteria.threshold: Game starts, renders, and accepts a movement command.

criteria.description: README or a docs file documents the dungeon generator: its tunable parameters, the glyph legend, and the `--seed` flag.
testSteps: Open README.md (or docs/) and search for sections referencing 'dungeon', 'seed', and the glyph legend.
criteria.threshold: All three topics (generator params, glyph legend, --seed flag) are documented.

outOfScope: Field of view / fog of war (Sprint 3).
Combat, HP, attack, defense, damage log (Sprint 3).
Permadeath, save/load, run summary, daily seeds (Sprint 6 — the `--seed` flag here is generation-only, not a full daily-seed system).
Themed archetypes and per-archetype palettes (Sprint 11) — Sprint 2 produces a single generic dungeon style.
Advanced generation (BSP, cellular automata caves, vaults, secret rooms). A simple rooms-and-corridors algorithm is sufficient.
deliverables: Dungeon generator module producing seedable rooms-and-corridors floors.
World/Floor abstraction supporting >=3 floors with up/down stair transitions.
Render loop displaying generated dungeon with the documented glyph set.
`--seed` CLI flag wired through to the generator.
Automated tests for determinism, connectivity, and stair invariants.
Updated README documenting generator params, glyph legend, and the `--seed` flag.
